Assembly: The assembly bEriRub2.2 is based on 41x PacBio data, 22x 10X Genomics Chromium data, and 42x Dovetail Hi-C data generated at the Wellcome Sanger Institute. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2, Arrow polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: Genome assembly of Holothuria poli. This assembly is provided by the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). Both haplotypes were curated, but only haplotype 2 (HAP2) was retained for this report.

Assembly: The assembly qcLitVari1.hap1.1 is based on 60x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The X chromosome could not be identified, due to lack of a comparator and the male genotype being XX.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Hesperioidea; Hesperiidae; Pyrginae; Erynnis
Assembly: The assembly ilEryTage1.1 is based on 29x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with HiCanu, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Viridiplantae; Streptophyta; Embryophyta; Tracheophyta; Spermatophyta; Magnoliopsida; eudicotyledons; Gunneridae; Pentapetalae; rosids; fabids; Fagales; Betulaceae; Carpinus
Assembly: The assembly dhCarBetu1.hap5.1 is based on 92x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This sample is octaploid with basal chromosome number of 8. We have generated 8 curated haplotypes. Note that due to software limitations unplaced content is split between haplotypes 1 and 2 and haplotypes 6 to 8 only contain assembled chromosomes.

Assembly: The assembly ilOxyUnic1.hap1.1 is based on 44x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was identified by BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (~https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4 )Chromosome W was identified by copy number in the diploid assembly.The order and orientation of scaffolds in the following regions is uncertain:Chromosome 4 between approximately 0-563KbChromosome 25 between approximately 0-1.33Mb and 16.78-17.17MbChromosome W between approximately 0-158.98Mb.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Coleoptera; Polyphaga; Staphyliniformia; Staphylinidae; Tachyporinae group; Aleocharinae; Lomechusini; Drusilla
Assembly: The assembly icDruCana1.hap1.1 is based on 154x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Haplotypic inversions were detected on Chromosome X from 3.86 to the end and on Chromosome 8 from 3.2 to 11.5 Mbp. The region on Chromosome 9 from 10.9 to the end is repetitive and the order and orientation may be uncertain. The X chromosome was identified based on BUSCO ancestral hits. Some centromeric regions look to be missing, they are not present in the assembly.

Assembly: The assembly dmThaMinu1.hap1.1 is based on 46x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The order and orientation of contigs in centromere associated regions is uncertain for all chromosomes.

Assembly: The assembly wpAliVire1.1 is based on 49x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Repetitive scaffolds have been inserted into a repeat region of chromosome 4. Their order and orientation is uncertain.
